title: microlicia hispidula
intro: Microlicia hispidula, also known as Brazilian tea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It is a shrub native to the tropical regions of South America and is valued for its medicinal and therapeutic properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain, fever, and inflammation, and may also aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as diabetes and hypertension. Microlicia hispidula can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to promote digestive health, reduce stress and anxiety, and support immune function.
